summary: mcp-server-pg@0.7.0 postinstall exfiltrates git/SSH/cloud identity to remote endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-side identity and credential-adjacent files — ~/.gitconfig and.git/config (git user.email),.git/logs/HEAD reflog (recent committer emails), ~/.ssh/*.pub (email comments from SSH public keys), ~/.aws/config (profile names and SSO identifiers), ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (GitHub CLI auth config), ~/.config/gcloud/properties (GCP account/project), /etc/resolv.conf (search domain), OS hostname, username, cwd, and the parent project's package.json name/author/repo — and POSTs the aggregated payload via https to a hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. The script is labeled as 'telemetry' / 'compatibility check', but the collection scope (installer secret directories not owned by this package) and hardcoded author-controlled destination make it credential-harvest exfiltration, not telemetry. Runs automatically at install time without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
